attempts.reason: The launcher stopped draining the event pipe before a verdict. The process was terminated after confirming it had only read files and had not mutated the review tree.

findings.title: Tar-shard header metadata lacks an aggregate allocation bound
findings.evidence: pipeline.rs reads one independently bounded metadata blob for every declared tar member and retains all decoded headers before checking the 256 MiB tar-body bound. The member-count ceiling is 1,048,576 and the per-item metadata ceiling is about 2.13 MiB; no shard-wide header budget exists.
findings.predicted_failure: A hostile peer can drive receiver memory toward terabytes before the existing body cap is reached; even a planner-sized 4096-file shard can request several GiB of header metadata.

findings.title: Unsupported destination attributes can cause endless successful re-transfers
findings.evidence: destination_matches compares the full preserved mask, apply_attributes checks only SetFileAttributesW success, and the result is not read back. A volume that accepts the call but drops a bit differs again on every later run.
findings.predicted_failure: Files on exFAT/FAT32/SMB can be fully retransferred on every run without converging or warning.

findings.title: A recoverable source ADS problem aborts the entire session
findings.evidence: The source scan records and skips ordinary PermissionDenied/NotFound files, but every metadata enumeration/read/cap/drift error propagates through `?` and terminates the scan. Destination comparison similarly propagates errors while inspecting pre-existing streams.
findings.predicted_failure: One locked, changing, oversized, or otherwise unreadable named stream prevents every other file in a backup from transferring.

findings.title: Windows-to-non-Windows transfers have no explicit downgrade and can reject after partial resume writes
findings.evidence: Every Windows file carries metadata, every non-Windows sink rejects it, no option disables carriage, and resume blocks can land before the completion record reaches the platform rejection.
findings.predicted_failure: Windows-to-Linux/macOS copies have no usable policy choice; a resume attempt can leave a partial destination before reporting the incompatibility.

findings.title: Local Windows metadata application truncates mtime to whole seconds
findings.evidence: The metadata branch stamps FileTime from FileHeader.mtime_seconds instead of the prior source SystemTime. Windows always supplies metadata, so the lossy branch shadows the nanosecond-preserving local path for every file.
findings.predicted_failure: NTFS sub-second timestamps do not round-trip and exact-time comparison tools report copied files as different.

findings.title: In-stream resume clones and discards every destination hash list
findings.evidence: The new metadata hydration wrapper clones hashes into TransferPayload::ResumeFile, then destructures only the hydrated header and continues using the original hashes. The clone occurs on metadata-free platforms too.
findings.predicted_failure: Large resumed files incur avoidable allocation and memory-copy churn on every platform.

guard.mutation: Removed the metadata-mismatch Transfer arm; windows_metadata_difference_overrides_an_ordinary_skip failed with Skip instead of Transfer.
guard.restoration: Exact reviewed bytes restored; cargo test -p blit-core --lib: 388 passed; detached worktree clean at reviewed_sha.
